title: flow.config.json
description: Per-project configuration for the flow plugin. Every slot is optional; documented defaults apply when unset. Schema for the flow plugin v1.1.0+.

defaultBranch.description: The branch that PRs target and that diffs are computed against. If unset, flow resolves via `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD`, then falls back to the literal `main`.

typecheckCmd.description: Shell command to run as the project's typecheck. Run via `sh -c` (NOT eval) by /flow:ship Step 3 (one-shot after reviewer fixes) and /flow:staff-review after applying review fixes. UNSET behavior: each invoking skill prints a loud ⚠️ warning, never silently no-ops. Project owns this string at the same trust level as package.json scripts or pre-commit hooks. Precedence vs `preflightCmd`: if both are set, BOTH run (preflightCmd at Step 1c with bounded retry; typecheckCmd at Step 3 one-shot after reviewer fixes). To use only one, set the other to empty or unset.

preflightCmd.description: Shell command for the project's mechanical preflight (typecheck + lint + fast tests; project owns the composition). Run via `sh -c` (NOT eval) at /flow:ship Step 1c and /flow:ship-spike Step 1c, BEFORE the reviewers run. On non-zero exit, the skill enters a bounded-retry loop: fix the failure, re-run, up to N=3 total invocations, with oscillation detection via diff-hash (same diff at attempt N as a prior attempt aborts the loop). Invoked ONLY when the diff contains non-doc files (per `sourceFilePatterns`); docs-only PRs skip preflight entirely. UNSET behavior: each invoking skill prints a loud ⚠️ warning and proceeds, never silently no-ops. Project owns this string at the same trust level as `typecheckCmd`. Design rationale: per Anthropic's evaluator-optimizer guidance, agent loops must have mechanical stopping conditions and externally-verifiable success criteria; preflight script exit code is the only loop-exit signal flow trusts (reviewer outputs at Step 2 are deliberately single-pass to prevent reward hacking). See dev-docs/feedback.md FB-0011.
examples: npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm test -- --bail

historyPath.description: Path (relative to repo root) to the project's history doc. /flow:ship writes new entries here. /flow:ship-spike writes the spike entry here (it IS the deliverable). Default: dev-docs/history.md (flow's own repo convention). Consumer projects typically set this to core-docs/history.md.

planPath.description: Path to the project's plan doc. Read by every skill at session start (Current Focus + Handoff Notes); written by /flow:ship (move shipped → Recently Completed; refresh Current Focus) and the planner agent (Active Work Items). Default: dev-docs/plan.md.

roadmapPath.description: Path to the project's roadmap doc. /flow:ship and /flow:staff-review route FOLLOW-UP findings here. Push-further lens routes future-exploration findings to the doc's § Exploration section. Default: dev-docs/roadmap.md.

specPath.description: Path to the project's spec doc (product vision, problem, solution, feature table). Read by planner, /flow:critique-plan, /flow:security-review (for context). Default: dev-docs/spec.md.

feedbackPath.description: Path to the project's user-feedback doc (FB-XXXX entries synthesized from user corrections / preferences / direction / review feedback). Written by /flow:ship step 4a; read by every reviewer for context. Default: dev-docs/feedback.md.

designLanguagePath.description: Path to the project's design-language doc (tokens, axioms, component conventions, motion principles). Optional — projects without a UI surface may not have one. Read by /flow:staff-review (UX + design-engineer + push-further lenses) and /flow:accessibility-review for grounding. Default: dev-docs/design-language.md.

referenceGlob.description: Glob pattern (relative to repo root) that /flow:critique-plan's preprocessor uses to load reference docs deterministically. Excludes history.md / plan.md / roadmap.md automatically (those churn too often to be reliable spec sources). Default: core-docs/*.md (the consumer-side convention). Flow's own repo overrides to dev-docs/*.md.

uiSurface.description: Whether this project ships a UI surface. If false, /flow:accessibility-review exits early with a clean 'no UI surface declared' message instead of running an empty audit. Default: true.

reviewLenses.description: Which lens agents /flow:staff-review spawns in parallel. Default is the 4-lens baseline; opt-in 'uncommon-care' (deeper standalone lens) when flow ships it in a future version. Removing a lens means /flow:staff-review won't spawn it; document the reason in the project's plan doc.

memoryHardCap.description: Maximum number of failure-pattern memory entries before tools/memory/check.mjs forces curation. At cap, no new entries can be written without archiving or merging existing ones. Default: 30. Tuning advice: revisit when it bites; if you find yourself wanting more, audit first (most projects need < 30 if the source-diversity bar is honored).

branchPrefix.description: Conventional prefix for feature branches (used by /flow:ship and /flow:ship-spike when they need to create a branch from main). Common values: 'claude/', 'feature/', 'fix/'. Empty string disables the prefix. Default: empty (flow doesn't add a prefix unless this is set).

platform.description: Optional project-platform hint consumed by /flow:verify-build for skip-path decisions. When set to 'library' or 'none', /flow:verify-build self-skips at Step 1.2 (no runnable target to verify). When unset (the default), bundled /run autodetects the platform via project structure (CLI / server / TUI / browser-driven / library) and /flow:verify-build accepts that autodetect. Set explicitly only when autodetect is wrong or you want the deterministic skip semantics for library/none. Other enum values (web/ios/android/tauri/cli) are informational hints rendered into findings-buffer metadata.platform_hint; they do NOT alter verify-build's launch behavior (bundled /run remains the authoritative launcher).

verifyEnabled.description: Project-wide opt-in/out for /flow:verify-build, the runtime behavioral-verification gate at /flow:ship Step 2. When true (default), /flow:verify-build runs as part of the final-pass reviews; when false, it self-skips with a clean message and the ship pipeline proceeds without the runtime gate. Set to false for projects where runtime verification is not meaningful. For library-only projects, setting `platform: "library"` is the more semantic opt-out (verify-build then skips at the platform check rather than the project-wide gate). Default: true.

verifyFindingsPath.description: Path where /flow:verify-build Step 8 writes its structured findings buffer. /flow:ship Step 4a reads this path to derive candidate FB-XXXX entries (via source-diversity bar). Default '/tmp/flow-verify-findings.json' so the buffer is auto-wiped at reboot. Override to a project-local path (e.g., '.flow/last-verify.json') if you want the buffer preserved across boots for inspection. Concurrent verify-build runs against the same path will clobber each other; single-run-per-ship is the v1 contract.

verifyBudgetCalls.description: Hard cap on tool-call count consumed by bundled /verify during /flow:verify-build Step 5. When exceeded, the verify pass aborts and unjudged verdicts are forced to Unknown (which gates ship per FB-0011). The cap is a fail-closed safety net against runaway verify runs that would otherwise consume the session budget. Default 60 calls — enough for a typical web app verification (~15-25 calls) with headroom; raise for iOS/Android (~30-50 calls) or projects with multi-step verification flows. Mechanical exit signal per FB-0012(a) — does not loop on judge output.

verifyReportPath.description: Path where /flow:verify-build renders its EPHEMERAL HTML walkthrough (the artifact the human opens at the merge gate) from the findings buffer, via lib/render-report.py. Default '/tmp/flow-verify-report.html' so it is auto-wiped at reboot and never committed (it is image-heavy and per-run; the durable visual record is a separate, curated artifact). Persisted screenshot frames are written to an 'assets/' dir alongside this path. Override to a project-local path only if you want the report preserved across boots for inspection. Regenerated every iteration; discarded after merge.

visualHistoryPath.description: Path (relative to repo root) to the project's DURABLE visual record — the committed `visual-history.html` that is the picture companion to `historyPath`. Distinct from `verifyReportPath` (the ephemeral per-run report): this file is curated, decision-centric, reverse-chronological, and grows over the project's life. Written by /flow:ship's distill step (§ 5c): when `uiSurface` is true AND that run's verify-build buffer carried a load-bearing visual decision (a `grounding` entry that changed the user's read, or a resolved this-iteration `open_question`), the step authors ONE curated entry, copies the cited frames into a sibling `visual-history-assets/` dir, and inserts it via lib/insert-visual-history.py. CREATED ON FIRST WRITE from the bundled skeleton (skills/ship/lib/visual-history-skeleton.html) — NOT scaffolded by bootstrap.sh, so non-UI projects (`uiSurface:false`) never get an empty doc. The distill step self-skips (with an explicit reason) when `uiSurface` is false, verify-build was skipped, or no visual decision was load-bearing this run. Default: core-docs/visual-history.html (the consumer-side convention; flow's own repo is uiSurface:false and never writes it).

rustWorkspaceDir.description: (Tauri / Rust stacks only.) Directory containing the project's Rust workspace (Cargo.toml). Used by the tauri-rust-ts stack overlay's preflight runner to know where to run `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy`, `cargo test`. Default: 'src-tauri' (the Tauri convention). Non-Tauri Rust projects typically set this to '.' (workspace at repo root) or to the workspace subdirectory.

sourceFilePatterns.description: Extended-regex pattern (passed to grep -E) matching source/config file paths in the diff. Used by /flow:security-review at Step 1 to early-exit when a diff contains no source files (e.g., docs-only PR). Default covers TS/JS/Python/Rust/Swift/Go/Ruby/Java/Kotlin/Shell + JSON/YAML/TOML + Terraform/SQL/proto/GraphQL + Dockerfile/Makefile literals (security-relevant config classes). Override for projects with non-standard file layouts (monorepos, language-specific source roots). Invalid regex falls back to default + emits loud warning rather than silently skipping the review.

uiFilePatterns.description: Extended-regex pattern (passed to grep -E) matching UI file paths in the diff. Used by /flow:accessibility-review at Step 1 to early-exit when a diff contains no UI files (e.g., docs-only PR in a uiSurface=true project). Default covers TSX/JSX/Vue/Svelte/Astro/MDX/CSS/SCSS/Sass/Less/HTML + njk/hbs/ejs templates (extension-only — the original path-prefix branch was redundant since any file matching the prefix-form also matched the extension-only form). Override to constrain to specific path roots (e.g., '^(apps/web|packages/ui)/.+\.(tsx|css)$') or add non-standard extensions. Invalid regex falls back to default + emits loud warning (caught via grep -E exit-2 detection).
